messages.content: Tokenization converts text into numerical token IDs that the model can process. Methods like BPE (Byte Pair Encoding) and SentencePiece learn a vocabulary by iteratively merging frequent character pairs. For example, 'unhappiness' might become ['un', 'happiness'] or ['un', 'happy', 'ness'] depending on the tokenizer. The vocabulary size and tokenization strategy affect model performance, context usage efficiency, and multilingual capability.

Temperature and top-p are sampling parameters that control randomness in text generation. Temperature scales the logits before softmax — low values (0.1) make the model deterministic and focused, high values (1.5) increase creativity and diversity. Top-p (nucleus sampling) truncates the probability distribution to only consider tokens whose cumulative probability reaches p (e.g., 0.9), dynamically adjusting the candidate pool size based on confidence.
